title: Integrate container image test scripts into CI
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'The tests under tests/container-images/ are currently'

1. Create `.github/workflows/verify-container-images.yaml` — a new single-job workflow triggered on push (main, stable/**) and pull_request that installs yq and runs 5 static test scripts sequentially: verify_build_images_workflow.sh, verify_deviation_comments.sh, verify_release_config.sh, verify_spdx_headers.sh, test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh. Follow existing conventions (SPDX header, SHA-pinned actions/checkout, permissions: contents: read, concurrency ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }} with cancel-in-progress only on PR, timeout-minutes, runs-on: ubuntu-latest).

2. Modify `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` — add a verification step/job after build-base-images that runs verify_python_base.sh and verify_venv_builder.sh with digest-tagged image refs from job outputs. Replace the inline PR smoke test (~lines 191–196) and the push-only smoke-test job (~lines 198–254) with verify_keystone.sh; push path retains docker pull from GHCR before invocation, PR path uses local --load image.

3. Fix test script issues — add assert_not_empty to tests/lib/assertions.sh; refactor verify_keystone.sh manual pass/fail block (lines 31–37) to use it and align default image tag with CI tags; fix stale uv version in verify_venv_builder.sh (0.6.3 → match Dockerfile); evaluate/harden sed-based extras extraction in verify_release_config.sh (lines 97–108).

4. Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md to document test integration, verification coverage, and the new workflow.

**Rationale:** Eight test scripts exist but zero are executed in CI, meaning container image structure validation, SPDX compliance checks, release config validation, and Dockerfile convention enforcement provide no automated protection. Bugs like the stale uv version expectation in verify_venv_builder.sh (tests for 0.6.3 while Dockerfile uses 0.10.8) would fail immediately if CI ran the scripts, proving the gap is real. Integrating these scripts catches regressions at PR time, replaces the limited inline smoke test (single --version check) with comprehensive verification (non-root user, no build tools, runtime packages), and ensures the existing test investment delivers value.

**Affected Areas:**
- .github/workflows/verify-container-images.yaml
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml
- tests/lib/assertions.sh
- tests/container-images/verify_keystone.sh
- tests/container-images/verify_venv_builder.sh
- tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh
- docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md

stories.title: CI runs static container image tests on every push and PR
stories.want: static test scripts (workflow structure, SPDX headers, deviation comments, release config, constraint overrides) to execute automatically in CI on push to main/stable/** and on pull requests
stories.so_that: regressions in Dockerfiles, release configuration, SPDX compliance, and workflow structure are caught before merge
criteria: A new workflow verify-container-images.yaml runs on push to main and stable/** branches and on pull_request events
The workflow runs verify_build_images_workflow.sh, verify_deviation_comments.sh, verify_release_config.sh, verify_spdx_headers.sh, and test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh sequentially
yq is installed before scripts that require it (verify_build_images_workflow.sh and verify_release_config.sh)
The workflow fails the CI run if any test script exits non-zero

stories.title: CI verifies base container images after build
stories.want: verify_python_base.sh and verify_venv_builder.sh to run against the just-built base images in the build-images workflow
stories.so_that: base image properties (Python version, user UID/GID, PATH, uv version, virtualenv, common packages) are validated on every build
criteria: A new verify-base-images job runs after build-base-images completes in build-images.yaml
verify_python_base.sh receives the digest-tagged python-base image ref from build-base-images outputs
verify_venv_builder.sh receives the digest-tagged venv-builder image ref from build-base-images outputs
The verify-base-images job blocks build-service-images (dependency chain: build-base-images -> verify-base-images -> build-service-images)

stories.title: CI replaces inline smoke test with comprehensive keystone verification
stories.want: verify_keystone.sh to replace both the inline PR smoke test and the push-only smoke-test job in build-images.yaml
stories.so_that: keystone image verification covers non-root user, no build tools, runtime packages, and keystone-manage --version instead of just --version
criteria: The inline PR smoke test step (docker run --rm IMAGE keystone-manage --version) is replaced with verify_keystone.sh invocation using the local --load image
The push-only smoke-test job is replaced with verify_keystone.sh invocation preceded by docker pull from GHCR
verify_keystone.sh checks: keystone-manage --version, runs as openstack user, no gcc/python3-dev/uv in image, runtime apt packages installed
The workflow fails if verify_keystone.sh exits non-zero

stories.title: Test library assertion helpers are complete and consistent
stories.want: assert_not_empty added to tests/lib/assertions.sh so all test scripts can validate non-empty values without manual pass/fail counting
stories.so_that: test scripts use consistent assertion patterns and avoid duplicate manual pass/fail logic
criteria: assert_not_empty function exists in tests/lib/assertions.sh with signature: assert_not_empty description value
assert_not_empty increments PASS when value is non-empty, FAIL when empty, matching existing assertion conventions
verify_keystone.sh manual pass/fail block for version output is refactored to use assert_not_empty

stories.title: Test scripts have correct expectations matching current codebase
stories.want: stale version expectations and default image tags in test scripts to be fixed so tests pass against current images
stories.so_that: tests reflect the actual state of the codebase and do not produce false failures in CI
criteria: verify_venv_builder.sh expects uv version 0.10.8 (matching COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.10.8 in Dockerfile)
verify_keystone.sh default image tag is updated to a CI-compatible format or the default is documented as local-only
verify_release_config.sh yq-based extraction is reviewed and confirmed robust (no sed-based fragility)

stories.title: New workflow follows existing CI conventions
stories.want: verify-container-images.yaml to follow the same conventions as ci.yaml and build-images.yaml
stories.so_that: all workflows are consistent and pass existing workflow verification tests
criteria: SPDX header present (lines 1 and 3 match existing pattern)
actions/checkout pinned to full SHA with version comment
permissions: contents: read at top level
concurrency group uses ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }} with cancel-in-progress only on PR
timeout-minutes set on all jobs
runs-on: ubuntu-latest

stories.title: Documentation reflects test integration
stories.want: docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md updated to document which tests run where and the new verify-container-images workflow
stories.so_that: contributors understand the verification coverage and can extend it when adding new services or releases
criteria: Documentation describes the new verify-container-images.yaml workflow with trigger events, job structure, and test list
Documentation updates the build-images.yaml section to reflect the verify-base-images job and verify_keystone.sh integration
Documentation describes the dependency chain: build-base-images -> verify-base-images -> build-service-images -> verify-service-images

requirements.description: The system SHALL create a new verify-container-images.yaml workflow that runs 5 static test scripts on push to main/stable/** and on pull_request
requirements.rationale: Static tests validate workflow structure, SPDX headers, release config, deviation comments, and constraint overrides without Docker

scenarios.name: Static tests run on PR
scenarios.when: a pull request is opened or updated
scenarios.then: verify-container-images.yaml triggers and runs all 5 static test scripts sequentially
and_then: each script uses tests/lib/assertions.sh and exits 0 on success, non-zero on failure

scenarios.name: Static tests run on push to main
scenarios.when: a commit is pushed to the main branch
scenarios.then: verify-container-images.yaml triggers and runs all 5 static test scripts
and_then: results are visible in the GitHub Actions UI

scenarios.name: Static tests run on push to stable branches
scenarios.when: a commit is pushed to a stable/** branch
scenarios.then: verify-container-images.yaml triggers and runs all 5 static test scripts
and_then: stable branch protection includes this workflow

requirements.description: The verify-container-images.yaml workflow SHALL install yq before running test scripts that require it
requirements.rationale: verify_build_images_workflow.sh and verify_release_config.sh depend on yq for YAML parsing

scenarios.name: yq available for workflow verification
scenarios.when: verify_build_images_workflow.sh executes
scenarios.then: yq is available in PATH and can parse YAML files
and_then: all yq-based assertions in the script succeed

scenarios.name: yq available for release config verification
scenarios.when: verify_release_config.sh executes
scenarios.then: yq is available in PATH and can parse extra-packages.yaml and source-refs.yaml

scenarios.name: Scripts not requiring yq still succeed
scenarios.when: verify_deviation_comments.sh, verify_spdx_headers.sh, or test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh executes
scenarios.then: the scripts succeed regardless of yq installation order

requirements.description: The verify-container-images.yaml workflow SHALL follow existing CI conventions: SPDX header, SHA-pinned actions, least-privilege permissions, concurrency control, timeout-minutes, and runs-on ubuntu-latest
requirements.rationale: Consistency with ci.yaml and build-images.yaml prevents convention drift and passes existing workflow verification tests

scenarios.name: SPDX header present
scenarios.when: the workflow file is inspected
scenarios.then: line 1 contains SPDX-FileCopyrightText and line 3 contains SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

scenarios.name: Actions pinned to SHA
scenarios.when: the workflow file is inspected
scenarios.then: all uses: directives reference 40-character hex SHAs with version comments (# vN)

scenarios.name: Concurrency control configured
scenarios.when: the workflow is triggered
scenarios.then: concurrency group is ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }} with cancel-in-progress only on pull_request
and_then: push events to main/stable do not cancel in-progress runs

requirements.description: The build-images.yaml workflow SHALL add a verify-base-images job after build-base-images that runs verify_python_base.sh and verify_venv_builder.sh with digest-tagged image refs
requirements.rationale: Base image verification catches Python version, user UID/GID, PATH, uv version, and virtualenv issues before service image builds consume them

scenarios.name: Base images verified with digest refs
scenarios.when: build-base-images job completes successfully
scenarios.then: verify-base-images job starts and passes python-base digest ref to verify_python_base.sh and venv-builder digest ref to verify_venv_builder.sh
and_then: both scripts receive the exact digest-tagged image built in the preceding job

scenarios.name: Base image verification failure blocks service builds
scenarios.when: verify_python_base.sh or verify_venv_builder.sh exits non-zero
scenarios.then: the verify-base-images job fails and build-service-images does not start
and_then: the workflow reports the specific verification failure

scenarios.name: Base image verification job follows conventions
scenarios.when: the verify-base-images job is inspected
scenarios.then: it has timeout-minutes, runs-on ubuntu-latest, and permissions contents: read, packages: read

requirements.description: The build-images.yaml workflow SHALL replace the inline PR smoke test and push-only smoke-test job with verify_keystone.sh
requirements.rationale: verify_keystone.sh provides strictly more coverage (non-root user, no build tools, runtime packages) than the single --version check

scenarios.name: PR path uses verify_keystone.sh with local image
scenarios.when: a service image is built on a pull request event with --load
scenarios.then: verify_keystone.sh runs against the locally loaded image (composite tag)
and_then: the inline docker run --rm IMAGE keystone-manage --version step is removed

scenarios.name: Push path uses verify_keystone.sh with GHCR image
scenarios.when: a service image is built and pushed on a push event
scenarios.then: a verify-service-images job pulls the image from GHCR then runs verify_keystone.sh
and_then: the separate smoke-test job is replaced

scenarios.name: Verification covers all keystone checks
scenarios.when: verify_keystone.sh runs against a keystone image
scenarios.then: it verifies: keystone-manage --version exits 0 with non-empty output, runs as openstack user, gcc/python3-dev/uv not present, runtime apt packages installed

requirements.description: tests/lib/assertions.sh SHALL include an assert_not_empty function matching existing assertion conventions
requirements.rationale: Eliminates manual pass/fail counting in verify_keystone.sh and provides a reusable assertion for non-empty value checks

scenarios.name: assert_not_empty passes for non-empty value
scenarios.when: assert_not_empty is called with a non-empty value
scenarios.then: PASS counter increments and 'PASS: description' is printed

scenarios.name: assert_not_empty fails for empty value
scenarios.when: assert_not_empty is called with an empty string
scenarios.then: FAIL counter increments and 'FAIL: description (expected non-empty value)' is printed

scenarios.name: verify_keystone.sh uses assert_not_empty
scenarios.when: verify_keystone.sh is inspected
scenarios.then: the manual if/else block for version output non-empty check is replaced with assert_not_empty

requirements.description: verify_venv_builder.sh SHALL expect uv version 0.10.8 matching the current Dockerfile
requirements.rationale: The test currently expects 0.6.3 while the Dockerfile copies from ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.10.8, causing guaranteed false failures

scenarios.name: uv version expectation matches Dockerfile
scenarios.when: verify_venv_builder.sh runs against a current venv-builder image
scenarios.then: the uv version check asserts 0.10.8 instead of 0.6.3
and_then: the test passes against images built from the current Dockerfile

scenarios.name: Test fails against images with wrong uv version
scenarios.when: verify_venv_builder.sh runs against an image with uv version != 0.10.8
scenarios.then: the assertion fails with expected vs actual output

requirements.description: verify_keystone.sh SHALL accept an image ref as first argument and use assert_not_empty for version output validation
requirements.rationale: The current default c5c3/keystone:28.0.0 may not match CI-built tags; the script must work both locally and in CI

scenarios.name: CI passes image ref as argument
scenarios.when: the CI workflow invokes verify_keystone.sh with a GHCR digest-tagged or composite-tagged image ref
scenarios.then: the script uses the provided image ref for all docker run commands

scenarios.name: Local execution uses reasonable default
scenarios.when: verify_keystone.sh is invoked without arguments
scenarios.then: the script uses a default image tag that works with local build instructions from docs

requirements.description: docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md SHALL be updated to document test integration and the new verify-container-images workflow
requirements.rationale: Contributors need to understand verification coverage to extend it when adding new services or releases

scenarios.name: New workflow documented
scenarios.when: a contributor reads docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md
scenarios.then: they find a section describing verify-container-images.yaml with trigger events, prerequisites (yq), and the list of 5 static test scripts

scenarios.name: Build-images test integration documented
scenarios.when: a contributor reads the build-images.yaml documentation
scenarios.then: the job dependency chain shows verify-base-images between build-base-images and build-service-images, and verify-service-images replacing smoke-test
and_then: the 'Adding a New Service' section mentions verification script creation

scenarios.name: Verification coverage summary
scenarios.when: a contributor wants to understand what tests cover
scenarios.then: a table or list maps each test script to what it validates and where it runs

requirements.description: The verify-base-images and verify-service-images jobs SHALL use GHCR login with least-privilege permissions and pull images by digest
requirements.rationale: Digest-tagged pulls guarantee the verification runs against the exact image built in the preceding job; least-privilege prevents accidental writes

scenarios.name: Docker pull uses digest ref
scenarios.when: verify-base-images job runs
scenarios.then: docker pull uses the digest-tagged refs from build-base-images outputs (ghcr.io/<owner>/python-base@sha256:<digest>)

scenarios.name: GHCR login provides pull-only access
scenarios.when: verification jobs authenticate to GHCR
scenarios.then: permissions include packages: read but not packages: write

scenarios.name: Push-path service verification pulls from GHCR
scenarios.when: verify-service-images job runs on push events
scenarios.then: docker pull retrieves the image by composite tag from GHCR before running verify_keystone.sh

tasks.title: Add assert_not_empty to tests/lib/assertions.sh (REQ-006)
tasks.description: Add assert_not_empty function to tests/lib/assertions.sh following the existing pattern (description, value params; increment PASS/FAIL; print result). Place it after the existing assert_eq function. Function signature: assert_not_empty description value. Passes when value is non-empty, fails when empty with message '(expected non-empty value)'.

tasks.title: Fix stale uv version in verify_venv_builder.sh (REQ-007)
tasks.description: In tests/container-images/verify_venv_builder.sh, update test_uv_version_is_pinned to expect 0.10.8 instead of 0.6.3. Change the echo description, the assert_contains call, and the assert_contains description string. The Dockerfile at images/venv-builder/Dockerfile uses COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.10.8.

tasks.title: Refactor verify_keystone.sh to use assert_not_empty and align default tag (REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In tests/container-images/verify_keystone.sh: (1) Replace the manual if/else block in test_keystone_manage_version (lines 35-40: 'if [ -n "$version" ]' block) with a single call to assert_not_empty 'version output is non-empty' "$version". (2) Keep the default IMAGE as c5c3/keystone:28.0.0 since it matches the local build instructions in docs/reference/container-images.md. The CI workflow will always pass an explicit image ref.

tasks.title: Update verify_build_images_workflow.sh for new job structure (REQ-001, REQ-004, REQ-005)
tasks.description: In tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh: (1) Update test_three_jobs_defined to expect 4 jobs: build-base-images, verify-base-images, build-service-images, verify-service-images (rename function to test_four_jobs_defined or similar). (2) Add test for verify-base-images job: needs build-base-images, has timeout-minutes, runs-on ubuntu-latest, permissions packages: read. (3) Update test_smoke_test_* functions to reference verify-service-images instead of smoke-test. (4) Remove tests for the old smoke-test job structure that will no longer exist. (5) Add test for verify-service-images job: needs build-service-images, has timeout-minutes.

tasks.title: Create .github/workflows/verify-container-images.yaml (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Create new workflow file .github/workflows/verify-container-images.yaml with: (1) SPDX header matching ci.yaml. (2) Triggers: push to main and stable/**, pull_request. (3) permissions: contents: read. (4) Concurrency: ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }} with cancel-in-progress on PR only. (5) Single job 'verify-static-tests' with runs-on: ubuntu-latest, timeout-minutes: 10. (6) Steps: checkout (SHA-pinned actions/checkout@de0fac2e...  # v6), install yq (via snap or wget), then run each of the 5 scripts sequentially: verify_build_images_workflow.sh, verify_deviation_comments.sh, verify_release_config.sh, verify_spdx_headers.sh, test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh.

tasks.title: Add verify-base-images job to build-images.yaml (REQ-004, REQ-010)
tasks.description: Add a new verify-base-images job to .github/workflows/build-images.yaml: (1) needs: [build-base-images]. (2) runs-on: ubuntu-latest, timeout-minutes: 10. (3) permissions: contents: read, packages: read. (4) Steps: checkout (SHA-pinned), login to GHCR, docker pull both digest-tagged base images from build-base-images outputs, run verify_python_base.sh with python-base-image digest ref, run verify_venv_builder.sh with venv-builder-image digest ref. (5) Update build-service-images needs to include verify-base-images: needs: [build-base-images, verify-base-images].

tasks.title: Replace PR smoke test and push smoke-test job with verify_keystone.sh in build-images.yaml (REQ-005, REQ-010)
tasks.description: Modify .github/workflows/build-images.yaml: (1) Replace the inline 'Smoke test (PR)' step in build-service-images with a step that runs 'bash tests/container-images/verify_keystone.sh "$IMAGE_REF"' using the composite tag, conditioned on github.event_name == 'pull_request'. (2) Replace the entire smoke-test job with a new verify-service-images job: needs [build-service-images], if github.event_name != 'pull_request', runs-on ubuntu-latest, timeout-minutes 10, permissions contents: read + packages: read, same matrix strategy. Steps: checkout (SHA-pinned), GHCR login, derive image ref (same tag derivation as current smoke-test), docker pull, run verify_keystone.sh with the pulled image ref. Use env vars for matrix values in run blocks (expression injection defense).

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md for test integration (REQ-009)
tasks.description: Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md: (1) Add a new top-level section 'Verification Workflows' or 'Test Integration' documenting verify-container-images.yaml: trigger events, yq prerequisite, list of 5 static scripts with what each validates. (2) Update the Jobs section to show 4 jobs in build-images.yaml: build-base-images -> verify-base-images -> build-service-images -> verify-service-images. Update the ASCII dependency diagram. (3) Update the verify-base-images job description: what it tests, how images are referenced by digest. (4) Replace smoke-test documentation with verify-service-images documentation. (5) Update 'Adding a New Service' section to mention creating a verification script under tests/container-images/. (6) Add a verification coverage table mapping scripts to what they validate.

test_specifications.story: CI replaces inline smoke test with comprehensive keystone verification
test_specifications.expected: The inline 'Smoke test (PR)' step should be replaced with verify_keystone.sh invocation passing the composite tag

test_specifications.story: CI replaces inline smoke test with comprehensive keystone verification
test_specifications.expected: The push-only smoke-test job should be replaced with a verify-service-images job running verify_keystone.sh after docker pull

test_specifications.story: Test scripts have correct expectations matching current codebase

test_specifications.story: CI runs static container image tests on every push and PR
test_specifications.expected: After build-images.yaml modifications, this test must still pass (verify job names in workflow match test expectations)
Documentation includes sections for verify-container-images.yaml workflow, verify-base-images job, and verify-service-images job replacing smoke-test

test_specifications.story: CI runs static container image tests on every push and PR
test_specifications.expected: yq should be installed via a reliable method (e.g., snap install yq or wget from GitHub releases) before test scripts execute

review_criteria: verify-container-images.yaml has SPDX header, SHA-pinned actions, permissions: contents: read, concurrency control, timeout-minutes, and runs-on ubuntu-latest matching existing conventions
All 5 static test scripts execute successfully when run manually against the current codebase (bash tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh, etc.)
verify_venv_builder.sh expects uv version 0.10.8 matching images/venv-builder/Dockerfile COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.10.8
verify_keystone.sh uses assert_not_empty from assertions.sh instead of manual pass/fail counting for version output check
build-images.yaml dependency chain is correct: build-base-images -> verify-base-images -> build-service-images -> verify-service-images (push) / inline verify_keystone.sh (PR)
All matrix values in run: blocks use env: variables instead of direct ${{ matrix.* }} interpolation (expression injection defense)
verify-base-images and verify-service-images jobs have permissions packages: read (not write) following least-privilege principle
docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md accurately reflects the new workflow structure and verification coverage
verify_build_images_workflow.sh tests are updated to match the new job structure (4 jobs instead of 3, verify-service-images instead of smoke-test)
No Dockerfile, release configuration, or apply-constraint-overrides.sh modifications (out of scope per elaboration)
implementation_notes:
Architecture: This feature adds CI integration for 8 existing test scripts across two workflows. The static tests (no Docker) run in a new lightweight workflow. The Docker-based tests integrate into the existing build-images.yaml as verification jobs between build stages.

Key decisions:
1. verify-container-images.yaml is a separate workflow (not merged into ci.yaml) because it tests container infrastructure, not Go code. Separate workflows provide clear signal in GitHub's check status.
2. verify-base-images is inserted between build-base-images and build-service-images to catch base image issues before they cascade. The dependency chain becomes: build -> verify -> build -> verify.
3. The push-path smoke-test job is renamed to verify-service-images for consistency with verify-base-images.
4. The PR-path inline smoke test is replaced in-place (same step location, same if condition) with verify_keystone.sh to maintain the local --load image pattern.
5. yq installation in verify-container-images.yaml uses the same snap/wget approach as the runner provides for build-images.yaml (ubuntu-latest has yq via snap or we install it).

Critical file changes:
- .github/workflows/verify-container-images.yaml (NEW): ~50 lines
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml (MODIFIED): add verify-base-images job (~30 lines), replace smoke test inline step and smoke-test job with verify_keystone.sh invocations (~20 lines net change)
- tests/lib/assertions.sh (MODIFIED): add assert_not_empty (~10 lines)
- tests/container-images/verify_keystone.sh (MODIFIED): replace manual pass/fail with assert_not_empty (~3 line change)
- tests/container-images/verify_venv_builder.sh (MODIFIED): 0.6.3 -> 0.10.8 (~2 line change)
- tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh (MODIFIED): update job name expectations for new structure
- docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md (MODIFIED): document new workflow, verification jobs, and coverage

Risks:
1. verify_build_images_workflow.sh tests will fail until build-images.yaml is updated (task 1.4 must align with tasks 2.2/2.3). Implementation order matters: update the test expectations BEFORE or SIMULTANEOUSLY with the workflow changes.
2. yq installation method must be reliable across GitHub Actions runner updates. Using the mikefarah/yq binary download is more robust than snap.
3. The smoke-test job name change may break GitHub branch protection rules if 'smoke-test' is a required check. This is a one-time manual fix in repository settings.

Patterns to follow:
- SPDX headers: match ci.yaml format exactly (Copyright 2026 SAP SE)
- Action pinning: use same SHA for actions/checkout as in existing workflows (de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6)
- Expression injection defense: all matrix values go through env: blocks, never direct ${{ }} in run: blocks
- Assertion library: follow existing assert_eq/assert_contains pattern (description, params; PASS/FAIL counters; echo output)
